title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z is increasingly struggling with loneliness, and dating app Hinge is stepping in as an unlikely source of support. Hinge announced a $1 million fund to help social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London put on free or affordable social events, expanding its One More Hour initiative globally.
bullets: Gen Z faces rising loneliness.
Hinge launched a $1 million fund for social groups.
Target cities: New York, Los Angeles, London.
Funds to support free or affordable social events for young people.
This expands Hinge’s One More Hour social impact initiative globally.
takeaways: Initiatives focused on youth social events may drive localized, event-related demand in major cities — monitor NYC, LA, and London activity.
Event-backed programs from tech platforms could create short-term needs for reliable connectivity and venue services in targeted neighborhoods.
Track similar platform-led social initiatives as indicators of where concentrated, demographic-specific service demand may emerge.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent understands your company's specific information. The presenters show an end-to-end build that requires no graph-query or graph-theory expertise.
bullets: Knowledge graphs remedy agents' lack of company-specific understanding.
Automated pipeline builds a knowledge graph from raw documents.
Connects the knowledge graph to an AI agent so it can use business data.
No prior graph expertise (queries or theory) is required.
End-to-end demo format — practical, hands-on build.
takeaways: Enable AI agents to access and reason over your organization’s documents for more relevant outputs.
Automated KG construction reduces the need to train staff in graph technologies.
An end-to-end approach can lower barriers to deploying enterprise-aware AI agents quickly.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum of PwC US highlights that energy tax credits are only part of the sustainability tax picture and that less obvious incentives—such as state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation like reimagining packaging or processes—also matter. The core message: viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax-incentive lens can unlock meaningful tax value.
bullets: Energy incentives are commonly top of mind but aren’t the whole story.
State and local abatements can support sustainability initiatives.
Location-based incentives can affect project economics and site choices.
R&D tax credits may apply to innovation efforts (e.g., reimagining packaging or processes).
A broader tax-incentive view can unlock meaningful tax value from sustainability investments.
takeaways: When evaluating grid or data center projects, include state and local abatements and location-based incentives in site-selection and project analysis.
Look for R&D tax credit opportunities tied to innovation in processes or technologies used in facility operations.
Analyze sustainability investments across federal, state, and local incentive programs to reveal additional tax value.
Build tax-incentive considerations into project ROI and decision-making for sustainability upgrades or new builds.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the short promotional clip shows Copilot helping Christa "find the star within," framing the assistant as a creative, personal aid. Viewers are invited to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Promotional video featuring Christa and Microsoft Copilot.
Theme centers on finding your "inner star" and personal creativity.
Ends with a clear call-to-action to download Copilot (link in bio).
No transcript is available for the video.
takeaways: Marketing pushes with a download CTA can drive user onboarding and increased demand on backend services.
Positioning Copilot as a personal/creative tool may produce varied usage patterns that affect capacity planning.
Monitor campaign-driven traffic and downloads closely to ensure adequate scaling and uptime.
Prepare onboarding, telemetry, and support pipelines to handle influxes from promotional content.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this video announces the winner of NVIDIA's Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest and presents u/JeffersonPutnam's small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. GeForce host Dwight walks through the build and links the full PCPartPicker parts list.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build from the Summer of RTX community contest.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Key components: MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, 32GB Teamgroup DDR5-6000 RAM.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 power supply and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Video hosted by GeForce’s Dwight; full parts list available on PCPartPicker (link in description).
takeaways: RTX 5090 Founders Edition is shown deployed in a small-form-factor desktop (Fractal Design Era 2).
Power provisioning for this build uses a 1000W SFX unit (Corsair SF1000) — note the PSU choice for high-end GPUs in compact systems.
Cooling for the GPU/CPU pair includes a Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO, highlighting active liquid cooling in SFF high-performance builds.
Platform details (MSI B850I Edge mini-ITX, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, DDR5-6000) indicate a high-frequency DDR5 plus high-core-count CPU pairing for GPU-heavy workloads.
This community showcase reflects active enthusiast adoption and available reference configuration details via the provided PCPartPicker list.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description… TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions on Google Cloud, compressing its digital transformation timeline. The engagement streamlined operations, boosted developer productivity, and unlocked entirely new revenue streams while expanding the company’s ambitions.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
Leveraged GenAI alongside Google Cloud’s infrastructure for faster development.
Engagement compressed digital transformation timelines.
Resulted in operational streamlining and higher developer productivity.
Enabled new revenue streams and expanded business possibilities.
takeaways: Cloud consulting can shorten transformation timelines—plan for faster AI rollouts.
Robust cloud infrastructure is key to prototyping and scaling GenAI workloads reliably.
Improved developer productivity from integrated tooling reduces time-to-market for services.
Rapid AI deployment can create new revenue opportunities—align infrastructure capacity to business objectives.
